approach: quickstart.py with high-expansion priors (R1-fitted) + 1-obs-per-cell tiling + observation blending

forest.root_cause: R6 settlements replaced forests more aggressively than R1 training data

plains_d0_3.root_cause: Expansion rate higher than training range

plains_d4_plus.root_cause: Far expansion underestimated — R6 had more chain expansion

ruin.root_cause: Floor 0.005 too low for ruin class in high-expansion rounds (GT had ~0.03)

lessons: R6 settle_rate=0.259 was beyond training range (max 0.214). Linear model extrapolated poorly.
Forest cells are MORE dynamic than priors assumed — settlements aggressively replace forests in high-expansion rounds.
Ruin floor should be ~0.02 not 0.005 in high-expansion rounds.
Observation-based rate detection (22% from queries) was close to true rate (26%) but priors were still R1-fitted.
Need to use continuous model coefficients applied to OBSERVED rate, not pre-fitted regime priors.
improvements_for_next_round: Use continuous rate model (Q46) with observed rate from queries
Increase ruin floor to 0.02 in high-expansion rounds
Train on R6 ground truth to expand training range to 0.26
Forest prior should scale more steeply with rate — forests are not as stable as assumed
Consider hybrid: static prior + simulator for spatial dynamics
